title: Dense Roasted Chinese Leaf Pho
description: A robust, comforting, and savory twist on the classic Vietnamese pho, featuring roasted Chinese leaf and mantecare-emulsified broth.
instructions: Preheat the oven to 1808C and season the pork shoulder with salt and black pepper, then roast in the oven for 1 hour.
Peel and slice the ginger, then roast in the oven for 1 hour.
Sear the pork shoulder in a hot pan until browned on all sides, then set aside.
In the same pan, saut60 the sliced ginger, then add the Gallagher's stock, mantecare, fish sauce, soy sauce, sesame oil, star anise, cloves, cinnamon stick, and cayenne pepper, and bring to a boil.
Reduce the heat to a simmer and cook for 30 minutes, then season with salt and sugar.
Slice the pork shoulder into thin strips and add to the broth, then cook for a further 10 minutes.
In a separate pan, sear the sliced scallions until caramelized, then add to the broth.
Add the Chinese leaf to the broth and cook until wilted, then serve hot, garnished with additional scallions and a sprinkle of black pepper.
Serve with steamed rice or noodles, if desired.
Ensure poultry is cooked to an internal temperature of 74°C / 165°F (juices run clear, no pink remaining) before serving.
Cook pork to an internal temperature of 63°C / 145°F and let it rest for 3 minutes before serving.
